Title: xAPI: Published content queries
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
xAPI endpoint accessible
Steps:
1. Add published content in admin > Check xAPI query returns data
2. Add Content Items to publishing > Check query
3. Set Is Active = False > Check query returns empty
4. Set Is Active = True > Check query returns data

4. Set Start Date > Check query

Title: Add coupon with validation
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend
Steps:
1. Navigate to Marketing > Promotions > Coupon management
2. Click 'Add'
3. Try to create without required fields > Verify validation
4. Fill all fields (code, max uses, max per user)
5. Click 'Create'

Admin logged in to Backend
Coupon exists [MKT-037]
Steps:
1. Edit coupon code > Save
2. Edit 'Maximum uses number' > Save

Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Coupons exist

Title: Import coupons (valid and invalid files)
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
CSV files prepared
Steps:
1. Import valid file > Add new coupons > Verify created
2. Import valid file > Update existing coupons > Verify updated
3. Import invalid: missing coupon_code column > Verify error
4. Import invalid: missing max_uses_number column > Verify error
5. Import invalid: wrong delimiter > Verify error
6. Import invalid: text in max_uses_number > Verify error

Title: Personal coupon assignment (organization/user/anonymous)
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend and Storefront
Personal coupon exists
Organization with members exists
Steps:
1. Assign coupon to organization > Check via org member > Verify works
2. Assign to organization > Check via anonymous > Verify not applied
3. Assign to organization > Check via non-member > Verify not applied
4. Assign to user > Check via that user > Verify works
5. Assign to user > Check via another user > Verify not applied
6. No assignment > Check via various users > Verify behavior

Title: Coupon applied in storefront
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend and Storefront
Coupon with promotion exists
Steps:
1. Add coupon code in storefront cart
2. Verify promotion is applied

Title: Coupon expiration and usage limits
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend and Storefront
Coupon exists
Steps:
1. Set expiration in past > Try on storefront > Verify not applied
2. Set expiration in future > Verify applied
3. Set uses < max uses > Verify applied
4. Set uses >= max uses > Verify not applied
5. Set uses per user > max per user > Verify not applied
6. Set uses per user < max per user > Verify applied

Title: xAPI Marketing Content Query — Query Active Content via GraphQL
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Active content publishing exists with Is Active = TRUE
xAPI endpoint accessible
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/marketing/dynamicContent and ensure content publishing is active
2. Open GraphQL playground or API client targeting the xAPI endpoint
3. Query for dynamic content items using the appropriate xAPI query
4. Verify the response includes the active content item
5. Set Is Active = FALSE on the content publishing in admin
6. Re-execute the GraphQL query
7. Verify the response does not include the now-inactive content
8. Re-enable Is Active = TRUE and verify content returns in query
9. Verify query results match the admin configuration exactly

Title: Create dynamic association (CRUD)
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
VirtoCommerce.DynamicAssociationsModule installed and active
Steps:
1. Navigate to Marketing > Dynamic Associations
2. Click 'Add' to open the new association blade
3. Enter Name (e.g., 'Cross-sell Electronics')
4. Set Group (e.g., 'Related Products')
5. Set Priority to 10
6. Verify 'aActive' toggle is ON by default (note: column header has known typo 'aActive')
7. Configure association rules: select source category and target category
8. Click 'Save'
9. Verify the association appears in the list grid with correct Name, Group, Priority, and Active status
10. Reopen the association blade and verify all fields persisted correctly

Title: Edit dynamic association
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Dynamic association exists [MKT-067]
Steps:
1. Navigate to Marketing > Dynamic Associations
2. Open an existing dynamic association from the grid
3. Edit the Name field to a new value (e.g., 'Updated Cross-sell')
4. Save and verify the name change persisted
5. Reopen and change the Group field to a different value
6. Save and verify
7. Reopen and change Priority from 10 to 50
8. Save and verify priority updated in the grid
9. Toggle Active state from ON to OFF
10. Save and verify the Active column reflects the change

Title: Delete dynamic association (single and bulk)
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Multiple dynamic associations exist
Steps:
1. Navigate to Marketing > Dynamic Associations
2. Select a single association via checkbox
3. Click 'Delete' toolbar button > Confirm deletion
4. Verify the association is removed from the grid
5. Create 3 new associations for bulk delete test
6. Select all 3 associations via checkboxes
7. Click 'Delete' toolbar button > Confirm bulk deletion
8. Verify all 3 associations are removed from the grid
9. Verify the total count in the grid updates correctly

Title: Search dynamic associations
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Multiple dynamic associations exist with different names
Steps:
1. Navigate to Marketing > Dynamic Associations
2. Enter a partial name in the search bar (e.g., 'Cross')
3. Verify the grid filters to show only matching associations
4. Enter the full exact name and verify single result
5. Enter a non-matching term (e.g., 'ZZZZNOTEXIST')
6. Verify the grid shows empty state or no results
7. Clear the search field and verify the full list is restored
8. Verify pagination works: if more than one page of results, navigate to page 2 and back

Title: Evaluate dynamic association rules via API
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Dynamic association with configured rules exists [MKT-067]
Products exist in the configured source and target categories
Steps:
1. Create a dynamic association with source category 'Electronics' and target category 'Accessories'
2. Set the association to Active and save
3. Send POST request to /api/dynamicassociations/evaluate with body:
   { "productId": "<source-product-id>", "group": "Related Products", "storeId": "${STORE_ID}" }
4. Verify HTTP 200 response with array of recommended product IDs from the target category
5. Send the same request with a productId NOT in the source category
6. Verify the response returns empty array or no matching associations
7. Set the association to Inactive and repeat step 3
8. Verify inactive association returns no results

Title: Preview dynamic association results via API
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Dynamic association with configured rules exists [MKT-067]
Steps:
1. Open an existing dynamic association in the admin blade
2. Send POST request to /api/dynamicassociations/preview with the association configuration payload
3. Verify HTTP 200 response with preview of matching products
4. Modify the association rules (change target category) without saving
5. Send another preview request with the modified payload
6. Verify preview results reflect the modified rules (not the saved state)
7. Send preview with invalid/empty rules
8. Verify graceful error handling (HTTP 400 or empty result, not 500)

Title: Priority ordering between dynamic associations
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Two dynamic associations exist for the same source category with different priorities
Steps:
1. Create Association A: source='Electronics', target='Accessories', Priority=10, Active=TRUE
2. Create Association B: source='Electronics', target='Cables', Priority=20, Active=TRUE
3. Send POST /api/dynamicassociations/evaluate for a product in Electronics
4. Verify results include products from both Accessories and Cables
5. Note the ordering of results: higher priority (lower number) association results should appear first
6. Change Association A Priority to 30 (lower priority than B)
7. Re-evaluate and verify Association B results now appear first
8. Set both associations to the same priority and verify both still return results

Title: Active/inactive toggle excludes from evaluate results
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Dynamic association exists [MKT-067] with Active=TRUE
Steps:
1. Navigate to Marketing > Dynamic Associations
2. Open the existing active association
3. Send POST /api/dynamicassociations/evaluate and confirm results are returned
4. Toggle the Active switch to OFF (inactive)
5. Save the association
6. Verify the 'aActive' column in the grid shows inactive state
7. Send the same POST /api/dynamicassociations/evaluate request
8. Verify the response returns no results from this association
9. Toggle Active back to ON and save
10. Re-evaluate and confirm results are returned again

Title: Delete store with assigned promotions
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Promotion assigned to a specific store exists
KNOWN BUG: 'Clothing store main page slider' content publishing has broken store reference showing 'Error: Not found'
Steps:
1. Navigate to Marketing > Promotions
2. Note a promotion assigned to a specific store (e.g., 'Electronics Store')
3. Navigate to Stores module and attempt to delete that store
4. Observe the behavior: does deletion succeed, fail, or warn?
5. If deletion succeeds: return to Marketing > Promotions
6. Open the promotion that was assigned to the deleted store
7. Verify the Store field behavior (empty, error, or graceful fallback)
8. Check for 'Error: Not found' similar to the known broken store reference in 'Clothing store main page slider'
9. Verify the promotion can still be edited and reassigned to another store

Title: Delete content placeholder linked to published content
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Content publishing exists with a linked placeholder [MKT-012]
Steps:
1. Navigate to Marketing > Dynamic Content > Content Publishing
2. Open an existing publishing and note the linked placeholder name
3. Navigate to Marketing > Dynamic Content > Placeholders
4. Delete the linked placeholder > Confirm
5. Return to the content publishing that referenced the deleted placeholder
6. Open the publishing blade and inspect the placeholder reference
7. Verify behavior: does it show empty, error, or orphaned reference?
8. Check storefront to verify no broken content renders
9. Verify the publishing can still be edited and a new placeholder assigned

Title: Delete content item referenced in publication
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Content publishing exists with linked content items [MKT-012]
Steps:
1. Navigate to Marketing > Dynamic Content > Content Publishing
2. Open an existing publishing and note the linked content item(s)
3. Navigate to Marketing > Dynamic Content > Content Items
4. Delete the linked content item > Confirm
5. Return to the content publishing that referenced the deleted content item
6. Open the publishing blade and inspect the content items section
7. Verify behavior: does it show empty list, error, or orphaned reference?
8. Query xAPI for the affected publishing's content
9. Verify xAPI returns empty content (not an error) for the affected publishing

Title: Delete promotion with active coupons
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Promotion with active coupons exists [MKT-037]
Steps:
1. Navigate to Marketing > Promotions
2. Open a promotion that has active coupons assigned
3. Note the coupon codes associated with this promotion
4. Delete the promotion > Confirm
5. Verify the promotion is removed from the list
6. Navigate to check if orphaned coupons remain (search for the coupon codes)
7. Try to use one of the deleted promotion's coupon codes on storefront
8. Verify the coupon is no longer valid

Title: Promotions search REST API contract
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Multiple promotions exist (32+ from exploration data)
API authentication token obtained
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/marketing/promotions/search with body: { "skip": 0, "take": 10 }
2. Verify HTTP 200 response with JSON containing 'results' array and 'totalCount'
3. Verify response schema: each promotion has id, name, store, isActive, startDate, endDate, priority
4. Verify totalCount matches the actual number of promotions in admin
5. Test pagination: send with skip=10, take=10 and verify next page of results
6. Test sorting: send with "sort": "name:asc" and verify results are alphabetically ordered
7. Test with skip=0, take=0 and verify response returns totalCount but empty results array
8. Test with skip exceeding totalCount and verify empty results with correct totalCount
9. Send request without authentication token and verify HTTP 401

Title: Content items evaluate REST API contract
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Content publishing exists with placeholder and content items [MKT-012]
API authentication token obtained
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/marketing/contentitems/evaluate with body:
   { "storeId": "${STORE_ID}", "placeholderName": "<existing-placeholder>", "cultureName": "en-US" }
2. Verify HTTP 200 response with array of matching content items
3. Verify each content item has expected fields: id, name, contentType, dynamicContent
4. Send request with a non-existent placeholder name
5. Verify response returns empty array (not 404 or 500)
6. Send request without storeId
7. Verify appropriate validation error (HTTP 400 or empty result)
8. Send request with cultureName for a language with no content
9. Verify response handles missing localized content gracefully

Title: Promotion CRUD via REST API
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
API authentication token obtained
Steps:
1. POST /api/marketing/promotions with body: { "name": "API Test Promo", "store": "${STORE_ID}", "isActive": true }
2. Verify HTTP 200/201 response with created promotion object including generated ID
3. GET the created promotion by ID and verify all fields match
4. PUT /api/marketing/promotions with updated name: "API Test Promo Updated"
5. Verify HTTP 200/204 response
6. GET the promotion again and verify name is updated
7. Test PUT idempotency: send the same PUT request again and verify same result (no duplicate)
8. DELETE /api/marketing/promotions with the promotion ID
9. Verify HTTP 200/204 response
10. GET the deleted promotion and verify HTTP 404
11. Repeat DELETE on the same ID and verify idempotent behavior (no error or 404)

Title: Coupon import API error handling
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
API authentication token obtained
Test CSV files prepared: valid file, invalid files (missing columns, wrong delimiter, text in numeric fields)
Steps:
1. POST /api/marketing/promotions/coupons/import with a valid CSV file containing coupon_code and max_uses_number columns
2. Verify HTTP 200 response and coupons are created
3. POST import with CSV missing the 'coupon_code' column
4. Verify appropriate error response (HTTP 400) with message about missing required column
5. POST import with CSV missing the 'max_uses_number' column
6. Verify appropriate error response about missing column
7. POST import with CSV using semicolon delimiter instead of comma
8. Verify error response about invalid format
9. POST import with CSV containing text values in 'max_uses_number' column
10. Verify error response about invalid data type
11. POST import with an excessively large file (1000+ rows)
12. Verify the import completes or returns a clear size limit error

Title: Dynamic associations evaluate REST API contract
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Dynamic association with configured rules exists [MKT-067]
API authentication token obtained
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/dynamicassociations/evaluate with body:
   { "productId": "<valid-product-id>", "group": "Related Products", "storeId": "${STORE_ID}", "take": 10 }
2. Verify HTTP 200 response with array of associated product IDs
3. Verify response schema includes expected fields (productId, associatedObjects or results array)
4. Send request with non-existent productId
5. Verify response returns empty array (not 404 or 500)
6. Send request with empty/null group parameter
7. Verify graceful handling (all groups returned or validation error)
8. Send request without storeId
9. Verify validation error response
10. Send request without authentication and verify HTTP 401

Title: xAPI query with invalid placeholder name
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
xAPI endpoint accessible at ${BACK_URL}/ui/graphiql
Steps:
1. Open GraphiQL at ${BACK_URL}/ui/graphiql
2. Execute a dynamic content query with a valid storeId but invalid/non-existent placeholder name (e.g., 'NONEXISTENT_PLACEHOLDER_999')
3. Observe the response: check for HTTP status code and response body
4. Verify the response is NOT an HTTP 500 Internal Server Error
5. Verify the response returns empty data or a descriptive error message
6. Execute the query with an empty string placeholder name
7. Verify graceful error handling (validation error or empty result)
8. Execute the query with special characters in placeholder name (e.g., '<script>alert(1)</script>')
9. Verify no XSS reflection and graceful error response

Title: xAPI query without storeId
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
xAPI endpoint accessible at ${BACK_URL}/ui/graphiql
Steps:
1. Open GraphiQL at ${BACK_URL}/ui/graphiql
2. Execute a dynamic content query WITHOUT the storeId parameter
3. Observe the response for validation error
4. Verify the error message clearly indicates storeId is required
5. Execute the query with an empty string storeId
6. Verify appropriate validation error
7. Execute the query with a non-existent storeId (e.g., 'FAKE_STORE_ID')
8. Verify the response returns empty data or store-not-found error
9. Verify no HTTP 500 errors in any scenario

Title: MarketingExperienceApi Swagger health check
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
KNOWN BUG: This endpoint currently returns HTTP 500
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/docs/VirtoCommerce.MarketingExperienceApi/swagger.json
2. Observe the HTTP response status code
3. If HTTP 200: verify the response is valid JSON with OpenAPI/Swagger schema
4. If HTTP 500: document the error response body
5. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/docs/index.html
6. Check if VirtoCommerce.MarketingExperienceApi appears in the Swagger UI module dropdown
7. If present: click to load and verify the API documentation renders
8. Check browser console for any JavaScript errors during Swagger load
